```{r Tree Plot Code}
          panel.background = element_blank(),
          plot.background = element_rect(fill = "white"),
          panel.border = element_blank(),
          axis.line = element_blank(),
          axis.text.x = element_blank(),
          axis.text.y = element_blank(),
          axis.ticks = element_blank(),
          axis.title.x = element_blank(),
          axis.title.y = element_blank(),
          plot.title = element_text(size = 18))
  
  print(plot)


```

```{r Gradient Boost}
#model 1: Method 1
  #https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/h2o/h2o.pdf
  #http://docs.h2o.ai/h2o/latest-stable/h2o-docs/performance-and-prediction.html#logloss
  #localH2O = h2o.init()
  #training_frame' must be a valid H2OFrame

#FUNCTION
require("h2o")
gradient=function(data=data, target="PURCHASE_FLAG", vars=varsp, seed=seed){
list=list()
X=2:length(vars)
y=1
seed = seed

ntrees_opts = seq(100, 500, 50)
max_depth_opts = seq(2, 20, 2)
sample_rate_opts = seq(0.1, 0.9, 0.1)
col_sample_rate_opts = seq(0.1, 0.9, 0.1)

hyper_params = list(
  ntrees = ntrees_opts,
  max_depth = max_depth_opts,
  sample_rate = sample_rate_opts,
  col_sample_rate = col_sample_rate_opts
  )

search_criteria = list(
        strategy = "RandomDiscrete",
        max_runtime_secs = 300,
        max_models = 75,
        stopping_metric = "AUC",
        stopping_tolerance = 0.0001,
        stopping_rounds = 5,
        seed = seed
        )

h2o.init()
data_f=data[,c(target,vars)]
data_h=as.h2o(data_f)
  
  splits=h2o.splitFrame(
  data_h,           ##  splitting the H2O frame we read above
  c(0.6,0.2),   ##  create splits of 60% and 20%; 
                ##  H2O will create one more split of 1-(sum of these parameters)
                ##  so we will get 0.6 / 0.2 / 1 - (0.6+0.2) = 0.6/0.2/0.2
  seed=seed)    ##  setting a seed will ensure reproducible results (not R's seed)

  train2=h2o.assign(splits[[1]], "train.hex")   
                ## assign the first result the R variable train
                ## and the H2O name train.hex
  valid2=h2o.assign(splits[[2]], "valid.hex")   ## R valid, H2O valid.hex
  test2=h2o.assign(splits[[3]], "test.hex")     ## R test, H2O test.hex
  
  #GB
  loan_gbm=h2o.grid(
        "xgboost",
        grid_id = "gbm_grid",
        x = 2:ncol(train2),
        y = 1,
        training_frame = train2,
        validation_frame = valid2,
        
        # per model stopping criteria
        stopping_rounds = 2,
        stopping_tolerance = 1e-3,
        stopping_metric = "AUC",
        
        # how often to score (affects early stopping, training speed)
        score_tree_interval = 5,
        
        # seed to control sampling
        seed = seed,
        # grid serach options
        hyper_params = hyper_params,
        search_criteria = search_criteria
        )
  
  sorted_grid=h2o.getGrid(grid_id = "gbm_grid")

  # select best model
  best_model =h2o.getModel(sorted_grid@model_ids[[1]])
  list$nrow=nrow(data_f)
  list$model=best_model
  list$valid=h2o.assign(splits[[2]], "valid.hex")
  return(list)
  h2o.shutdown(prompt = FALSE)

}

#VARS for PURCHASE
varsp=c("SESSIONS_CONV_60","sku_ratio_to_class","review_count_group","REVIEW_COUNT","REVIEW_SCORE","DEVICE_TYPE","EVENT_FLAG","ON_SALE_FLAG","DISTRICT_NAME","BRAND_CAT","GENDER","USA_FLAG","CHINA_FLAG","STORE_FLAG","AGE_CATEGORY","SEASON","ON_SALE_FLAG")

varsi=c("SESSIONS_VIEWED_60","sku_ratio_to_class","price_ratio_to_class","REVIEW_COUNT","REVIEW_SCORE","DEVICE_TYPE","EVENT_FLAG","AGE_CATEGORY","DISTRICT_NAME","BRAND_CAT","GENDER","USA_FLAG","CHINA_FLAG","ON_SALE_FLAG")


#RUN MODEL FUNCTION
best_model_tents_a=gradient(data=tents_balanced_a, target="ATC_FLAG", vars=varsp, seed=8136)
save(best_model_tents_a, file="best_model_tents_a.RData")
h2o.shutdown(prompt = FALSE)

best_model_womens_a=gradient(data=womens_balanced_a, target="ATC_FLAG", vars=varsp, seed=8136)
save(best_model_womens_a, file="best_model_womens_a.RData")
h2o.shutdown(prompt = FALSE)

best_model_footwear_a=gradient(data=footwear_balanced_a, target="ATC_FLAG", vars=varsp, seed=8136)
save(best_model_footwear_a, file="best_model_footwear_a.RData")
h2o.shutdown(prompt = FALSE)

best_model_mens_a=gradient(data=mens_balanced_a, target="ATC_FLAG", vars=varsp, seed=8136)
save(best_model_mens_a, file="best_model_mens_a.RData")
h2o.shutdown(prompt = FALSE)

#imp=h2o.varimp(best_model)
#h2o.partialPlot(best_model_boots_p_v2$model, as.h2o(best_model_boots_p_v2$valid), "sku_ratio_to_class")

#VARS for INTERACTION
varsi=c("SESSIONS_VIEWED_60","sku_ratio_to_class","price_ratio_to_class","REVIEW_COUNT","REVIEW_SCORE","DEVICE_TYPE","EVENT_FLAG","AGE_CATEGORY","DISTRICT_NAME","BRAND_CAT","GENDER","USA_FLAG","CHINA_FLAG","ON_SALE_FLAG")
  


  
###########################################################
    
  imp=imp[1:20,]
  save(imp,file = paste0(label,"_gbImportance.RData"))
  
  h2o.varimp_plot(best_model, num_of_features = 20)+ title(xlab=paste0(label,"_gbImportance"))
  
  h2o.partialPlot(best_model, valid2, "SESSIONS_VIEWED_60")+ title(main="SESSIONS_VIEWED_60")
h2o.partialPlot(best_model, valid2, "sku_ratio_to_class")+ title(main="price_ratio_to_dept")
h2o.partialPlot(best_model, valid2, "REVIEW_COUNT")+ title(main="REVIEW_COUNT")
h2o.partialPlot(best_model, valid2, "DEVICE_TYPE")+ title(main="DEVICE_TYPE")
h2o.partialPlot(best_model, valid2, "REVIEW_SCORE")+ title(main="TOTAL_SKU_COUNT")

h2o.partialPlot(best_model, valid2, "PRODUCT_PAGE_COLOR_COUNT")+ title(main="PRODUCT_PAGE_COLOR_COUNT")
h2o.partialPlot(best_model, valid2, "PRODUCT_PAGE_SIZE_COUNT")+ title(main="PRODUCT_PAGE_SIZE_COUNT")
h2o.partialPlot(best_model, valid2, "price_ratio_to_class")+ title(main="price_ratio_to_class")

  
  top_five=imp[1:5,"variable"]
  tryCatch({h2o.partialPlot(best_model, valid2, top_five[1])+ title(main=paste0(label,"_gbImportance")) }, warning = function() {w}, error = function(e) {})
  tryCatch({h2o.partialPlot(best_model, valid2, top_five[2])+ title(main=paste0(label,"_gbImportance")) }, warning = function(w) {}, error = function(e) {})
  
  tryCatch({h2o.partialPlot(best_model, valid2, top_five[3])+ title(main=paste0(label,"_gbImportance")) }, warning = function(w) {}, error = function(e) {})
  
  tryCatch({h2o.partialPlot(best_model, valid2, top_five[4])+ title(main=paste0(label,"_gbImportance")) }, warning = function(w) {}, error = function(e) {})
  
  tryCatch({h2o.partialPlot(best_model, valid2, top_five[5])+ title(main=paste0(label,"_gbImportance")) }, warning = function(w) {}, error = function(e) {})

  h2o.shutdown(prompt = FALSE)


# A perfect classifier has AUC = 1 and a completely random classifier has AUC = 0.5 (evaluate how well a binary classification model is able to distinguish between true positives and false positives)
# A Gini index of zero expresses perfect equality (or a totally useless classifier)
# Logloss can be any value greater than or equal to 0, with 0 meaning that the model correctly assigns a probability of 0% or 100%.
# MSE (average of the squares of the errors or deviations) - the lower the MSE the higher the accuracy of prediction
# RMSE sensitive to outliers

```

#################################################################################################
BACKUP:





##########################################################################
X=2:length(varsp)
y=1
seed = 932675

#labelsp=c("boots_balanced_p","camp_balanced_p","cycle_balanced_p","men_balanced_p","women_balanced_p")
#labelsi=c("boots_balanced_i","camp_balanced_i","cycle_balanced_i","men_balanced_i","women_balanced_i")

#load("~/Research/Results on Nav/men_balanced_i.RData")
# define random grid search parameters
ntrees_opts = seq(100, 500, 50)
max_depth_opts = seq(2, 20, 2)
sample_rate_opts = seq(0.1, 0.9, 0.1)
col_sample_rate_opts = seq(0.1, 0.9, 0.1)

hyper_params = list(
  ntrees = ntrees_opts,
  max_depth = max_depth_opts,
  sample_rate = sample_rate_opts,
  col_sample_rate = col_sample_rate_opts
  )

# search a random subset of these hyper-parmameters
# max runtime and max models are enforced
# and the search will stop after not improving much over the best 5 random models
search_criteria = list(
        strategy = "RandomDiscrete",
        max_runtime_secs = 300,
        max_models = 75,
        stopping_metric = "AUC",
        stopping_tolerance = 0.0001,
        stopping_rounds = 5,
        seed = seed
        )
require("h2o")
## Run models
#for(i in 2:10){
  h2o.init()
  #label=labelsp[i]
  #load(paste0("~/Research/Results on Nav/",label,".RData")
       
  data=data_balanced_p[,c("PURCHASE_FLAG",varsp)]
  data=as.h2o(data)
  
  splits=h2o.splitFrame(
  data,           ##  splitting the H2O frame we read above
  c(0.6,0.2),   ##  create splits of 60% and 20%; 
                ##  H2O will create one more split of 1-(sum of these parameters)
                ##  so we will get 0.6 / 0.2 / 1 - (0.6+0.2) = 0.6/0.2/0.2
  seed=seed)    ##  setting a seed will ensure reproducible results (not R's seed)

  train2=h2o.assign(splits[[1]], "train.hex")   
                ## assign the first result the R variable train
                ## and the H2O name train.hex
  valid2=h2o.assign(splits[[2]], "valid.hex")   ## R valid, H2O valid.hex
  test2=h2o.assign(splits[[3]], "test.hex")     ## R test, H2O test.hex
  
  #GB
  loan_gbm=h2o.grid(
        "xgboost",
        grid_id = "gbm_grid",
        x = 2:ncol(train2),
        y = 1,
        training_frame = train2,
        validation_frame = valid2,
        
        # per model stopping criteria
        stopping_rounds = 2,
        stopping_tolerance = 1e-3,
        stopping_metric = "AUC",
        
        # how often to score (affects early stopping, training speed)
        score_tree_interval = 5,
        
        # seed to control sampling
        seed = seed,
        # grid serach options
        hyper_params = hyper_params,
        search_criteria = search_criteria
        )
  
  sorted_grid=h2o.getGrid(grid_id = "gbm_grid")

  # select best model
  best_model =h2o.getModel(sorted_grid@model_ids[[1]])
  
  # use variable importance to get insight into important relationships
  #imp=h2o.varimp(best_model)
  h2o.shutdown(prompt = FALSE)
  
}
